add_ons

Load Packages

Code 
pacman::p_load(igraph,visNetwork,tidyverse,knitr)

Load Data

Code 
load("data/mc1_unpacked_data.RData")

Generate the overall graph

Code 
edges <- df_communications %>%
  filter(!is.na(responding_to)) %>%
  select(
    from = responding_to, 
    to = message_id, 
    label = message_type,       
    type = message_type
  ) %>%
  mutate(color = case_when(
    type == "broadcast" ~ "gray",
    type == "one_on_one_chat" ~ "blue",
    TRUE ~ "black"
  ))

# 2. Define the Nodes (Agents, Actions, and Decisions)
nodes <- df_communications %>%
  select(
    id = message_id,
    agent = agent_id,              # Corrected column name
    role = agent_role,             # Added for extra tooltip context
    message_type = message_type,
    content = content,             # Corrected column name
    channel = channel,
    deliberating = internal_state_deliberating
  ) %>%
  distinct(id, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(
    # PROXY LOGIC: Adjust these based on how you define a "decision" and a "fail"
    # Assuming a decision involves deliberation, and the fail is the public post
    is_decision = !is.na(deliberating), 
    embargo_fail = (channel == "official_post" | message_type == "public_post"), 
    
    # Visual mappings for the interactive dashboard
    shape = ifelse(is_decision, "diamond", "dot"),
    color = ifelse(embargo_fail, "red", "lightblue"),
    title = paste0(
      "<p><b>Agent:</b> ", agent, 
      "<br><b>Role:</b> ", role, 
      "<br><b>Channel:</b> ", channel,
      "<br><b>Action:</b> ", content, "</p>"
    ) 
  )


edges_clean <- edges %>%
  filter(from %in% nodes$id & to %in% nodes$id)

# 3. Create the igraph object 
event_graph <- graph_from_data_frame(d = edges_clean, vertices = nodes, directed = TRUE)

visIgraph(event_graph) %>%
visOptions(highlightNearest = TRUE, nodesIdSelection = TRUE)

Subgraph Analysis

Top 5 Critical Events/Decision Points

Code 
sc_scores <- subgraph_centrality(event_graph)

# 2. Bind the scores back to your original nodes data frame
nodes_with_analysis <- nodes %>%
  mutate(
    subgraph_centrality = sc_scores[id],
    # Scale the scores between 10 and 50 so they map beautifully to node sizes in visNetwork
    value = 10 + 40 * (subgraph_centrality - min(subgraph_centrality)) / 
                      (max(subgraph_centrality) - min(subgraph_centrality))
  )

# 3. Identify the Top 5 Critical Events/Decision Points
top_critical_events <- nodes_with_analysis %>%
  arrange(desc(subgraph_centrality)) %>%
  head(5) %>%
  select(id, agent, message_type, channel, subgraph_centrality, content)

# Print the top contributors to the console
print("--- TOP 5 CRITICAL SUBGRAPH CENTRALITY EVENTS ---")
[1] "--- TOP 5 CRITICAL SUBGRAPH CENTRALITY EVENTS ---"

Code 
# 1. Clean data preparation
vis_nodes <- nodes_with_analysis %>%
  mutate(
    label = id,
    # Standardize values to prevent extreme scaling physics issues
    value = log(subgraph_centrality + 1) * 5 
  )

vis_edges <- edges_clean %>%
  mutate(
    arrows = "to",
    smooth = FALSE # CRITICAL: Curved/smooth lines drastically slow down rendering. Set to FALSE.
  )

# 2. Build High-Performance Network
visNetwork(vis_nodes, vis_edges, width = "100%", height = "700px") %>%
  # Configure pre-rendering physics
  visPhysics(
    solver = "forceAtlas2Based",
    forceAtlas2Based = list(
      gravitationalConstant = -30,
      centralGravity = 0.01,
      springLength = 50,
      avoidOverlap = 0.5
    ),
    # Pre-compute the network layout for 150 iterations before showing it
    stabilization = list(
      enabled = TRUE,
      iterations = 150,
      updateInterval = 25 # Shows progress bar instead of frozen screen
    )
  ) %>%
  # FREEZE THE GRAPH ONCE CALM
  # This stops the ongoing simulation, removing 100% of the ongoing lag
  visEvents(stabilizationIterationsDone = "function () { this.setOptions({ physics: false }); }") %>%
  visOptions(
    highlightNearest = list(
      enabled = TRUE, 
      degree = 1,      
      hover = FALSE # CRITICAL: Change hover to FALSE. Real-time hover triggers a re-draw that causes micro-stutters.
    ),
    nodesIdSelection = TRUE,
    selectedBy = "agent"
  ) %>%
  visInteraction(
    navigationButtons = TRUE,
    hideEdgesOnDrag = TRUE,  # Hides complex lines while you pan/zoom to keep FPS high
    hideNodesOnDrag = FALSE
  ) %>%
  visLayout(
    randomSeed = 42,
    improvedLayout = FALSE # Disables an intensive pre-calculation pass
  )

Upgraded Ver

Code 
library(dplyr)
library(igraph)
library(visNetwork)

# ==========================================
# 1. METRICS & WEIGHTS CALCULATION
# ==========================================

# Compute Edge Weights
edges_weighted <- edges_clean %>%
  group_by(from, to, type, color) %>%
  summarise(weight = n(), .groups = 'drop') %>%
  mutate(
    smooth = FALSE,
    arrows = "to",
    value = weight, # Edge thickness maps to communication frequency
    title = paste("Edge Weight (Freq):", weight)
  )

# Calculate Network Centrality Metrics
sc_scores <- subgraph_centrality(event_graph)
bc_scores <- betweenness(event_graph, directed = TRUE)

# Build Comprehensive Analytics Node Table
nodes_analytics <- nodes_with_analysis %>%
  mutate(
    subgraph_score  = round(sc_scores[id], 2),
    betweenness_score = round(bc_scores[id], 2),
    label = id,
    # Normalize node sizing for high performance
    value = log(subgraph_score + 1) * 5,
    # Update HTML tooltip to display all calculated metrics right on hover
    title = paste0(
      "<div style='font-family: sans-serif; padding: 5px;'>",
      "<b>Message ID:</b> ", id, "<br>",
      "<b>Agent ID:</b> ", agent, "<br>",
      "<b>Role:</b> ", role, "<br><hr>",
      "<b>Subgraph Centrality:</b> ", subgraph_score, "<br>",
      "<b>Betweenness Centrality:</b> ", betweenness_score, "<br><hr>",
      "<b>Action:</b> ", content,
      "</div>"
    )
  )

# ==========================================
# 2. GENERATE HIGH-PERFORMANCE VISUALIZATION
# ==========================================

network_view <- visNetwork(nodes_analytics, edges_weighted, width = "100%", height = "800px") %>%
  visPhysics(
    solver = "forceAtlas2Based",
    stabilization = list(enabled = TRUE, iterations = 120)
  ) %>%
  # Freeze physics instantly after stabilization to completely eliminate lag in the RStudio pane
  visEvents(stabilizationIterationsDone = "function () { this.setOptions({ physics: false }); }") %>%
  visOptions(
    highlightNearest = list(enabled = TRUE, degree = 1, hover = FALSE),
    nodesIdSelection = TRUE,
    selectedBy = "agent"
  ) %>%
  visInteraction(hideEdgesOnDrag = TRUE, navigationButtons = TRUE) %>%
  visLayout(randomSeed = 42, improvedLayout = FALSE)

# Render the graph inside your RStudio Viewer Pane
network_view
Code 
# Compute Edge Weights for this view
edges_weighted <- edges_clean %>%
  group_by(from, to, type, color) %>%
  summarise(weight = n(), .groups = 'drop') %>%
  mutate(
    smooth = FALSE,
    arrows = "to"
  )

# Keep only the weak components that contain an embargo failure (red node)
components <- components(event_graph, mode = "weak")

nodes_with_components <- data.frame(
  id = V(event_graph)$name,
  component_id = components$membership
) %>% 
  left_join(nodes_with_analysis, by = "id") # FIXED variable name here

# Identify component IDs that have an embargo failure
critical_component_ids <- nodes_with_components %>%
  filter(embargo_fail == TRUE) %>%
  pull(component_id) %>%
  unique()

# Filter our tables using the clean consistent names
interpretable_nodes <- nodes_with_analysis %>%
  filter(id %in% nodes_with_components$id[nodes_with_components$component_id %in% critical_component_ids])

interpretable_edges <- edges_weighted %>%
  filter(from %in% interpretable_nodes$id & to %in% interpretable_nodes$id)

# Render Using the Left-To-Right Causal Tree
visNetwork(interpretable_nodes, interpretable_edges, width = "100%", height = "800px") %>%
  visHierarchicalLayout(
    direction = "LR",        
    sortMethod = "directed", 
    nodeSpacing = 150,       
    levelSeparation = 200
  ) %>%
  visPhysics(enabled = FALSE) %>% 
  visOptions(
    highlightNearest = list(
      enabled = TRUE, 
      degree = 1, 
      algorithm = "all" 
    ),
    nodesIdSelection = TRUE,
    selectedBy = "agent"
  ) %>%
  visInteraction(navigationButtons = TRUE) %>%
  visLayout(randomSeed = 42)
